regions.Adamawa: Highland plateau region, capital Ngaoundéré. Known for cattle-herding Fulani/Mbororo traditions, cool climate, and the Ranch de Ngaoundaba area.
regions.Centre: Capital Yaoundé. Home to the Beti-Pahuin peoples (Ewondo, Bulu, Eton). Political and administrative heart of Cameroon.
regions.East: Capital Bertoua. Dense rainforest, home to Baka communities and Dja Faunal Reserve (UNESCO World Heritage site).
regions.Far North: Capital Maroua. Sahelian landscapes, Mandara Mountains, Rhumsiki Peak, Kirdi and Mafa traditions, Waza National Park.
regions.Littoral: Capital Douala, the economic capital. Sawa/Duala coastal culture, home of the Ngondo Festival.
regions.North: Capital Garoua. Savannah plains, Benoue and Faro National Parks, Fulani royal courts (lamidats).
regions.Northwest: Capital Bamenda. Grassfields kingdoms (fondoms), Anglophone region, cool highland climate.
regions.West: Capital Bafoussam. Bamileke and Bamum peoples, Foumban Royal Palace, Toghu textile craftsmanship, Nguon Festival.
regions.South: Capital Ebolowa. Equatorial forest, Bulu-Fang traditions, Kribi beaches, Lobé Falls (waterfall meeting the ocean).
regions.Southwest: Capital Buea. Mount Cameroon (highest peak in West/Central Africa), Anglophone heritage, Limbe coastline, plantations.

festivals.name: Ngondo Festival
festivals.about: Sawa/Duala festival honoring water spirits (Miengu) along the Wouri River, with canoe races and ancestral ceremonies.

festivals.name: Nguon Festival
festivals.about: Bamum festival in Foumban celebrating the sultan and Bamum history, involving public dialogue and reconciliation ceremonies.

festivals.name: Medumba Cultural Days
festivals.about: Celebration of Bangangté/Medumba language and Bamileke heritage.

festivals.name: Feast of Lamibe
festivals.about: Gathering of Fulani traditional rulers (lamibe) in Garoua.

cuisine.about: Bitter leaves simmered with groundnut paste, served with fish, shrimp, or beef; associated with the Sawa people, eaten nationwide.
Cocoyam pounded into a starchy base, served with a yellow palm-oil-based soup; Northwest specialty.
Steamed pudding made from black-eyed peas and palm oil, wrapped in leaves.

ethnic_groups.Bamileke: One of the largest groups, West Region, organized into ~100 traditional chiefdoms (fondoms) each led by a fon. Known for Toghu textiles, beadwork, Medumba/Ghomala' languages.
ethnic_groups.Bamum: West Region, centered on Foumban, with their own historic script (Shumom/Bamum script) and royal palace.
ethnic_groups.Sawa/Duala: Littoral coastal peoples, celebrate the Ngondo Festival.
ethnic_groups.Fulani: Present across Adamawa, North, Far North; historically cattle-herding, Islamic royal courts (lamidats).
ethnic_groups.Baka: Forest peoples of the East Region, traditionally semi-nomadic hunter-gatherers with rich oral and musical traditions.

West.name: West
West.description: The West Region is one of the most culturally rich regions of Cameroon. It is known for the Bamileke and Bamum kingdoms, traditional chiefdoms, handicrafts, and cultural festivals.
West.history: The region has been home to powerful traditional kingdoms for centuries. The Bamum Kingdom, founded in the 14th century, became one of the most influential kingdoms in Central Africa.

centre.name: Centre
centre.description: The Centre Region is the political and administrative heart of Cameroon and home to the capital city Yaoundé.
centre.history: The region became the administrative center during German colonization before remaining the national capital under French administration and after independence.

Adamawa.name: Adamawa
Adamawa.description: Adamawa is known for its high plateaus, cattle breeding and Fulani culture.
Adamawa.history: The region developed under Fulani influence during the 19th century with Islamic lamidats.

East.name: East
East.description: The East Region is the largest region of Cameroon and is covered by dense equatorial rainforest.
East.history: The region has long been inhabited by forest communities including the Baka people.

South.name: South
South.description: The South Region is famous for its tropical forests, beaches and rich biodiversity.
South.history: The region has historically been inhabited by Bulu and Fang peoples.

North.name: North
North.description: The North Region is characterized by vast savannahs and Fulani traditions.
North.history: The region became an important center of Fulani Islamic kingdoms during the 19th century.

Far north.name: Far North
Far north.description: The Far North is known for its Sahel landscapes, mountains and unique traditional architecture.
Far north.history: The region has been influenced by numerous kingdoms and Islamic sultanates.

Littoral.name: Littoral
Littoral.description: The Littoral Region is Cameroon's economic center and home of the Sawa people.
Littoral.history: Douala became a major trading port during German colonial rule.

North west.name: North West
North west.description: The North West Region is famous for its cool climate, fondoms and Anglophone heritage.
North west.history: The region contains many traditional kingdoms ruled by Fons.

South west.name: South West
South west.description: The South West Region combines mountains, rainforest and Atlantic coastline.
South west.history: The region was part of British Southern Cameroons before reunification with the Republic of Cameroon.
